stack01Content:
# Main Stack — Team Profile

_5-man ranked stack | Last updated: 2026-04-03_

---

## Roster

| Callsign | Tracker | Role | Y10S4 Rank | RP |
|----------|---------|------|------------|-----|
| Grant | Eh_Grant | IGL / Flex | Emerald I | 3906 |
| Peej | Eh_Peej | Entry Fragger | Platinum IV | 3106 |
| Hound | Eh_Hound | Support | Emerald II | 3813 |
| Smigs | Eh_tooten | Support | Emerald III | 3743 |
| Sarge | Eh_SiegeyBodeez | Utility / Flex | Platinum II | 3390 |

_⚠️ Roster change 2026-03-21: Slug moved to B Team, Peej promoted to main stack._

⚠️ **Callsign/username note:** Sarge = Eh_SiegeyBodeez, Smigs = Eh_tooten. Some older scripts and docs have these swapped. Always trust the tracker username column.

---

## Solo vs. Group Assignment

| Player | Solo Push | Group Push | IGL | Utility |
|--------|-----------|------------|-----|---------|
| Grant  | ✅ Best   | ✅ Yes     | ✅ Primary | ✅ |
| Peej   | ⚠️ Can   | ✅ Best    | ❌ | ✅ |
| Sarge  | ⚠️ Can   | ✅ Best    | ⚠️ Sub-group lead | ✅ |
| Smigs  | ❌ Avoid  | ✅ Best    | ❌ | ✅ |
| Hound  | ❌ Avoid  | ✅ Yes     | ❌ | ✅ Best |

---

## Attack Formations

See STACK_03_ATTACK_STRATS.md for site-specific strats. Formations are blueprints, not rigid rules — audibles are expected based on read of the round.

### 3-1-1 (Default)
Three-man push + two solo players flanking from opposite sides.
- 3-man group: Peej leads entry, Sarge (sub-IGL) + Smigs or Hound support
- Solo 1: Grant (backside, rotational disruption)
- Solo 2: remaining support (Hound or Smigs)
- Best for: Maps with multiple viable entry points, crossfire opportunities

### 3-2
Two coordinated groups — 3 and 2 — collapsing on site simultaneously.
- 3-man: Sarge / Smigs / Hound
- 2-man: Grant / Peej (Peej opens entries, Grant exploits the space)
- Best for: Sites where timing is critical and defense gets caught between two threats
- Requires highest coordination of any formation

### 2-2-1
Two strike teams of 2 + one solo player. Slow, methodical map control.
- Solo: Grant or Peej
- Best for: Maps where map control dominates and rushing is punished

### 4-1
Heavy coordinated rush on one front + one distraction solo.
- Solo: Flex / situational
- Best for: Time-crunch situations. Use sparingly.

### 5-0
Full 5-man singular push. High risk, high reward.
- Best for: Rare, high-stakes moments. Must be called in advance by IGL.

---

## Team Identity

### Attack
Utility-heavy with dedicated entry. Peej (Ash/Brava — 1,205r Ash all-time) fills the entry fragger role the team previously lacked. Structure is now: Peej forces entries mechanically, Sarge/Hound/Smigs provide hard breaching and utility support, Grant exploits space from backside.

**Strength:** Coordinated utility and breach setups. Formation discipline. IGL quality (Grant). Dedicated entry fragger (Peej — Ash primary, 1,205r all-time, legitimate entry archetype).
**Weakness:** Entry depth — if Peej is eliminated early, no secondary mechanical entry fragger. Team falls back on utility-attack identity.

**Key note:** Sarge's role updated to Utility/Flex — his ATK pool (Thermite/Sledge/Thatcher/Buck) is breach-support by identity. With Peej filling the entry fragger role, this is now the intended team structure rather than a gap.

### Defense
Anchor-dominant with a defined structure:
- **Intel anchor / pick:** Peej (Caveira — 72.9% WR all-time / Maestro — 73.7% WR all-time)
- **Post-plant denial / area control:** Peej (Maestro — camera network + LMG denial combo)
- **Trapper/early picks:** Grant (Kapkan — 45r, 53.3% WR in Y11S1) + Grant (Thorn)
- **Zone control:** Smigs (Kaid — 60.0% WR Y11S1) + Sarge (Vigil — 78r, 59.0% WR) + Sarge (Azami — 42r, 57.1%)
- **Utility wall anchor:** Hound (Azami — 22r, 68.2% ⭐ Y11S1) + Hound (Castle/Bandit)

**Strength:** Very hard to crack when coordinated. Multiple threat layers (traps, smoke denial, intel, anchor fire). All 5 players have positive DEF win rates.
**Weakness:** Communication-dependent — without callouts, individual players make defensive reads in isolation.

---

## Key Team Dynamics

**Grant and Peej are the individual carries** — both above-baseline K/D (1.19 and 1.21 Y11S1) and most adaptable. Grant handles IGL + backside solo; Peej provides the mechanical entry opening. Formation assignments should give Peej space to create first picks and protect Grant for mid-to-late round execution.

**Smigs and Hound are group players.** Both are most effective as part of a coordinated 3-man push where their utility (breach, gadget clear, callouts) enables the fragger. Neither should be in solo entry scenarios.

**Sarge is the sub-group IGL.** When the 3-man group needs leadership, Sarge leads it. His coordination capability makes him the right choice for group execution — he's most effective leading the 3-man while Grant takes the backside solo and Peej leads entry.

**The team wins on defense, loses rounds on attack.** This is the primary coaching priority. All 5 players have strong DEF stats; attack K/Ds and win rates are consistently lower. Improving attack win rate by even 5–10% would directly translate to more round wins and rank gains.

---

## Map Strategy

_⚠️ Y11S1 data has completely reversed the Y10S4 map picture. See STACK_05_MAP_VETO.md for full breakdown. Updated 2026-04-03 (145/117/91/81/39M)._

**Default ban (Y11S1):** Emerald Plains (universal 0% WR across all five players — hard ban, no exceptions)
**Second ban:** Oregon or Nighthaven are both primary candidates. Oregon is now confirmed weak — 4 of 5 players below 50%, Sarge 28.6%/14M and Hound 30%/10M at scale. Nighthaven remains severe — Hound 14.3%/7M is the anchor. Consulate is also now a full ❌ Avoid (all five players 20–27%, 5–11M each).
**Team strengths to protect:** Clubhouse (all five 62–80%), Border (all five 50–78%), Chalet (all four with data 55–60%)
**Villa is no longer a ban target:** Grant 53.8%/13M and Peej 83.3%/6M are clearly positive — do not waste a ban. Sarge/Hound/Smigs are below 45% but the map is situationally playable.
**Confirmed weaknesses to avoid:** Oregon, Consulate, Nighthaven, Lair (all 20–25%), Fortress (all 25–40% small samples)
**Major surprise:** Chalet was the team's hardest ban based on all-time/Y10S4 data — Y11S1 shows it's now one of the strongest maps. Do not ban.

---

## Team Focus

_Team-wide coaching themes for session prep. These apply to the whole stack regardless of roster lineup._

1. **Attack win rate is the gap.** The team wins on defense and loses rounds on attack — all 5 players have strong DEF stats. Every session, identify at least one ATK-side adjustment (timing, formation, or operator) to close the gap. Even a 5–10% improvement in ATK WR translates directly to rank gains.
2. **Protect your best maps in the ban phase.** Clubhouse, Chalet, and Border are the current strength maps. Don't give them up in the ban phase. Let the opponent ban their own bad maps — your job is to protect your wins.
3. **Entry timing is a team action, not a solo one.** Peej leads entry with Ash — but entry only works when breach detonation and utility are deployed first. Hard breach goes before Ash pushes. If Peej is dying on entry, ask whether breach/utility preceded the push.
4. **Defense is communication-dependent.** When callouts break down, each player makes defensive reads in isolation and the defense collapses. Prioritize active comms through plant phase and post-plant. The team's DEF strength comes from coordination, not just individual anchors.
5. **Emerald Plains is a hard ban — no exceptions.** Universal 0% WR across all five players. Do not negotiate it away in the ban phase regardless of opponent tendencies. This is the team's unambiguous worst map.
6. **Oregon and Consulate are now confirmed team-wide weaknesses.** Oregon: 4 of 5 players below 50% with Sarge (28.6%/14M) and Hound (30%/10M) confirmed at scale. Consulate: all five players at 20–27% with 5–11M each — the most conclusive team-wide signal in the pool. Treat both as priority Ban 2 candidates. Villa is no longer a ban target — Grant (53.8%) and Peej (83.3%) are both clearly positive there.

---

## Priority Coaching Items

1. ✅ ~~**ATK-side entry fragger gap**~~ — Resolved 2026-03-21. Peej promoted to main stack; Ash entry fragger role filled. Team ATK identity is now utility-heavy with dedicated entry (Peej leads, Sarge/Hound/Smigs support, Grant exploits).
2. ✅ ~~**Chalet ban commitment**~~ — Resolved Y11S1. Chalet is now one of the team's strongest maps (Grant 62.5%, Sarge 62.5%, Hound 75%, Peej 50%). Do not ban. Protect it in the ban phase.
3. **Sarge rank gap** — conversation about solo queue vs. stack performance discrepancy
   _→ KB: [V1.T14, V6.T10, V8.T10] FFA warmup habit (solo-queue cold-start delta); [V1.T09] Role Consistency (solo queue may be exposing op pool gap)_
4. **Peej ATK op balance (Y11S1)** — Ash (65r / 38.5%) is the volume pick but underperforming relative to season expectations. Brava (33r / 54.5%) is outperforming Ash by a wide margin with a meaningful sample. Worth experimenting with Brava as the default ATK pick on maps where DEF gadget destruction is valuable, rather than defaulting to Ash every round. Also note Twitch (55r / 43.6%) — confirm the team is getting value from the drone-disable intel role, not just using it as a generic soft-breacher. Caveira DEF (90r / 64.4%) is elite — protect that role.
   _→ KB: [V1.T09] Role Consistency (Peej — revisit Ash vs. Brava priority); [V4.T05, V10.T07] Push space after kills (entry fragger must take the space they open)_
5. **Hound ATK op pool (Y11S1)** — All top-6 ATK ops are underperforming: Thermite (40r / 35%), Osa (38r / 36.8%), Nomad (28r / 32.1%), Fuze (23r / 39.1%), Thatcher (22r / 41.4%), Zero (22r / 45.5%). Rauora (15r / 53.3%) and Gridlock (11r / 72.7%) are the clear bright spots. Consider shifting volume toward Rauora as a primary ATK pick — the sample is meaningful and the WR gap is significant. ATK improvement is the biggest lever for Hound's overall performance.
   _→ KB: [V2.T04] Op selection discipline; [V4.T06, V10.T10] Zone-based DEF — Azami (29r / 62.1%) and Frost (26r / 57.7%) outperforming Castle (53r / 49.1%) on DEF — worth trying as default before defaulting to Castle_
6. ✅ ~~Smigs DEF #4 repull~~ — Resolved 2026-03-06. Nomad moved to ATK; confirmed DEF #4 is Thorn (35r, 57.1% WR, 0.91 K/D)

---

## Stack vs. B Team Comparison (Y11S1 — as of 2026-04-03)

| Player | Rank | RP | K/D | Win% |
|--------|------|----|-----|------|
| Grant | Emerald I | 3,900 | 1.19 | 52.4% |
| **Peej** | **Emerald V** | **3,514** | **1.21** | **53.1%** |
| Hound | Emerald IV | 3,619 | 0.99 | 48.4% |
| Smigs | Gold III | 2,755 | 1.10 | 53.8% |
| Sarge | Platinum IV | 3,100 | 1.04 | 50.4% |
| Slug | Platinum IV | 3,100 | 1.02 | 40.3% | *(B Team as of 2026-03-21)* |
| Hunter | Bronze III | 1,709 | 0.64 | 36.8% |
| Bob | Silver II | 2,354 | 0.69 | 35.7% |
| Krafty | Silver V | 2,017 | 0.35 | 49.5% |

_⚠️ Roster change 2026-03-21: Peej promoted to main stack (entry fragger gap filled). Slug moved to B Team. Bold = main stack; remainder = B Team._
_Stats sourced from Y11S1.md files pulled 2026-04-03. Match counts: Grant 145M, Sarge 117M, Hound 91M, Peej 81M, Smigs 39M, Slug 62M, Krafty 99M, Bob 56M, Hunter 19M._
stack05Content:
# Map Veto Guide — Y11S1

_Last updated: 2026-04-26 | Repulled 2026-04-26 (178/99/104/60/132M). Major rating changes from 2026-04-03: Border ✅ Protect (was ✅ Strong — now team's top map), Clubhouse ✅ Strong (was ✅ Protect — Grant/Peej drifted toward break-even), Bank ⚠️ Moderate (was ✅ Strong — Grant 38.9%/18M, Smigs 16.7%/6M confirmed weak)._

---

## How This File Works

The ban decision always comes down to three factors: **win rate data**, **how comfortable the team feels on a map at ban time**, and **how frequently the map gets played** (frequency affects how practiced the team is). This file handles the first factor with verified data. The comfort and frequency reads are made at the table — they override data when the team consensus is strong.

---

## Y11S1 Pool — Both Halves

### First Half Launch (March 2026)
Bank, Border, Chalet, Clubhouse, Coastline, Consulate, Fortress, Kafe Dostoyevsky, Lair, Nighthaven Labs, Oregon, Villa, Emerald Plains _(13 maps)_

### Mid-Season Swap
- **OUT:** Coastline, Villa, Oregon, Emerald Plains
- **IN:** Skyscraper, Theme Park, Stadium Bravo, Favela

### Second Half Pool
Bank, Border, Chalet, Clubhouse, Consulate, Fortress, Kafe Dostoyevsky, Lair, Nighthaven Labs, Skyscraper, Theme Park, Stadium Bravo, Favela _(13 maps)_

---

## Map Ratings — Default 5-Stack (Y11S1 First Half)

_Ratings reflect team-wide Y11S1 data pulled 2026-04-26 (Grant 178M, Sarge 132M, Hound 104M, Peej 99M, Smigs 60M). ⚠️ Roster change 2026-03-21: Peej replaced Slug in main stack. All five current stack players included below. ⚠️ Rating changes from 2026-04-26: Border upgraded to ✅ Protect, Clubhouse downgraded to ✅ Strong, Bank downgraded to ⚠️ Moderate._

| Map | Rating | Y11S1 Data |
|-----|--------|------------|
| **Border** | ✅ Protect | Grant 68.2% (22M) / Sarge 66.7% (15M) / Hound 60.0% (15M) / Peej 83.3% (12M) / Smigs 50.0% (6M) — ⚠️ Rating change from ✅ Strong. Four of five players at 60%+; Peej at 83.3% is the top individual result on any map. Large samples across the board — team's best confirmed map |
| **Clubhouse** | ✅ Strong | Grant 52.9% (17M) / Sarge 66.7% (12M) / Hound 54.5% (11M) / Peej 50.0% (10M) / Smigs 80.0% (5M) — ⚠️ Rating change from ✅ Protect. Grant and Peej have drifted toward break-even (was 66.7%/62.5%); Sarge and Smigs still strong. Still a team strength — just no longer the dominant map it was at 39M |
| **Chalet** | ✅ Strong | Grant 60.0% (15M) / Sarge 61.5% (13M) / Hound 66.7% (6M) / Peej 54.5% (11M) / Smigs 66.7% (3M⚠️) — all five players positive; consistent across meaningful samples; genuine team strength. Smigs sample small but in line with the pattern |
| **Bank** | ⚠️ Moderate | Grant 38.9% (18M) / Sarge 41.7% (12M) / Hound 71.4% (7M) / Peej 58.3% (12M) / Smigs 16.7% (6M) — ⚠️ Rating change from ✅ Strong. Hound and Peej are elite here; Grant has dropped to 38.9% over 18M and Smigs is 16.7% over 6M now (sample meaningful). IGL below 40% is a real drag — Bank is no longer a team protect |
| **Kafe Dostoyevsky** | ⚠️ Moderate | Grant 50.0% (12M) / Sarge 50.0% (6M) / Hound 66.7% (6M) / Peej 42.9% (7M) / Smigs 0.0% (2M⚠️) — Hound carrying; Grant improved to 50% but Peej below baseline; Smigs sample still too small to weight. Not a protect |
| **Coastline** | ⚠️ Situational | Grant 40.0% (20M) / Sarge 70.0% (10M) / Hound 46.7% (15M) / Peej 30.0% (10M) / Smigs 50.0% (4M) — split: Sarge elite (70%), Grant/Peej both confirmed weak at real volume; leaves mid-season anyway. Not worth protecting |
| **Villa** | ⚠️ Situational | Grant 50.0% (14M) / Sarge 40.0% (10M) / Hound 37.5% (8M) / Peej 71.4% (7M) / Smigs 37.5% (8M) — Peej (71.4%) and Grant (50%) are positives; Sarge/Hound/Smigs all below 40%. Not a team-wide strength — Peej's result keeps it off the ban list but this is now leaning toward avoid territory for the group |
| **Oregon** | ❌ Avoid | Grant 44.4% (18M) / Sarge 26.7% (15M) / Hound 30.0% (10M) / Peej 33.3% (9M) / Smigs 66.7% (3M⚠️) — four of five below 50% with meaningful samples; Sarge 26.7%/15M is the anchor of the problem. Smigs positive but sample too small to offset |
| **Consulate** | ❌ Avoid | Grant 36.4% (11M) / Sarge 30.8% (13M) / Hound 16.7% (6M) / Peej 16.7% (6M) / Smigs 42.9% (7M) — all five players below 50%; Hound and Peej both at 16.7%. Smigs is the closest to even (42.9%) but still below baseline. Team-wide confirmed weakness |
| **Emerald Plains** | ❌ Hard ban | Grant 0% (5M) / Sarge 0% (5M) / Hound 0% (3M) / Peej 0% (3M) / Smigs 0% (2M) — universal 0% WR across all five players; ATK side collapse every player |
| **Nighthaven Labs** | ❌ Avoid | Grant 37.5% (16M) / Sarge 33.3% (12M) / Hound 12.5% (8M) / Peej 50.0% (10M) / Smigs 50.0% (6M) — Hound is a severe anchor (12.5%/8M confirmed); Grant and Sarge both drifted lower this pull. Peej and Smigs holding at 50% but the team drag is too significant |
| **Fortress** | ❌ Avoid | Grant 40.0% (5M) / Sarge 40.0% (5M) / Hound 25.0% (4M) / Peej 50.0% (2M⚠️) / Smigs — (no data) — small samples across the board; Hound still weak; not enough evidence to change the rating |
| **Lair** | ❌ Avoid | Grant 20.0% (5M) / Sarge 25.0% (4M) / Hound 20.0% (5M) — no Peej or Smigs Y11S1 data; all three players with data at 20–25%; remains a clear avoid |

---

## Default Ban Logic — 5-Stack (First Half)

### Ban 1: **Emerald Plains**
No exceptions. Universal 0% WR across all five players. This has replaced Chalet as the hard ban — Chalet is now a strong map.

### Ban 2: Best of these options at ban time
Listed in priority order:

- **Oregon** → primary Ban 2 candidate — Sarge 26.7%/15M and Hound 30%/10M are the confirmed anchors; Grant (44.4%/18M) and Peej (33.3%/9M) also below baseline. Four of five confirmed weak at meaningful samples
- **Nighthaven** → strong Ban 2 candidate — Hound 12.5%/8M (now confirmed catastrophic); Grant dropped to 37.5%/16M and Sarge to 33.3%/12M this pull. Peej and Smigs at 50% but can't carry the team drag
- **Consulate** → confirmed team-wide weakness — all five players below 50%; Hound and Peej both at 16.7%/6M. If Oregon and Nighthaven are already banned, Consulate is the next call
- **Fortress or Lair** → lower priority than the three above, but valid if those are already off the table

### Maps to protect (avoid using as bans)
**Border and Chalet** are the team's strongest Y11S1 maps — protect both. **Clubhouse** is still a solid team map even with the drift; don't waste a ban on it. **Villa is not a default ban** — Peej (71.4%) anchors the case for keeping it in the pool; Bank has softened but Hound/Peej are still elite there.

---

## Map Ratings — Default 5-Stack (Y11S1 Second Half)

_Mid-season swap: Coastline, Villa, Oregon, Emerald Plains OUT → Skyscraper, Theme Park, Stadium Bravo, Favela IN. All-time data used for returning maps since Y11S1 in-season data for these maps is limited or zero._

| Map | Rating | Notes |
|-----|--------|-------|
| **Clubhouse** | ✅ Protect | Same as first half — strong for all 5 players in Y11S1 |
| **Chalet** | ✅ Strong | Same as first half — now a genuine team strength in Y11S1 |
| **Border** | ✅ Strong | Unchanged |
| **Theme Park** | ✅ Strong | All-time: Sarge 60.5%, Hound 65.2%, Smigs 57.6%, Slug 52.4% *(B Team as of 2026-03-21; Peej data pending)* — 4 of 5 players positive; welcome return |
| **Kafe Dostoyevsky** | ⚠️ Moderate | Unchanged — directionally positive but uneven |
| **Bank** | ⚠️ Moderate | Y11S1 data was good (Hound 80%, Slug 66.7% *(B Team)*) but small samples; current lineup: Hound 80%, Peej 80% — both elite here |
| **Skyscraper** | ⚠️ Moderate | All-time: Slug 52.2% *(B Team as of 2026-03-21; Peej data pending)*, Hound 62.5% — Sarge 37.5% and Grant 44.4% are soft spots |
| **Stadium Bravo** | ⚠️ Unknown | New map — no stack data. Take reps and build plan before committing either way |
| **Favela** | ⚠️ Unknown | New map — no stack data. Same as above |
| **Consulate** | ⚠️ Weak | Unchanged — multiple soft spots |
| **Nighthaven Labs** | ❌ Avoid | Unchanged — team-wide confirmed weakness |
| **Fortress** | ❌ Avoid | Y11S1 data shows 20–25% WR for all players — do not treat as a strong map based on all-time numbers |
| **Lair** | ❌ Avoid | Y11S1 data shows 0–25% WR for all players — do not treat as a strong map based on all-time numbers |

### Second Half Ban Adjustments
- **Ban 1 is Nighthaven** (was Emerald Plains in first half — Emerald Plains leaves at swap). Nighthaven remains a confirmed team-wide weakness: Hound 14.3%/7M and Sarge 36.4%/11M are the anchors.
- **Ban 2 options:** Consulate is now the primary alternative — all five players at 20–27% with 5–11M each. Lair (20–25% for all three players with data) and Fortress (still weak, small samples) are next. Oregon leaves at the swap so it's no longer a factor.
- **New maps (Stadium Bravo, Favela):** In the first few sessions, consider banning one unknown while the team gets reps. Prioritize getting reps on at least one before committing to always banning it — the ban only applies while it's the worst option.
- **Theme Park and Skyscraper return:** Theme Park is likely a keep (strong all-time). Skyscraper needs Grant and Sarge to build reps — all-time data shows a split (good for Peej/Hound, soft for Grant/Sarge).

---

## Scenario: Slug Fills In (B Team + Main Stack Mix)

_Slug Y11S1 data: 107M as of 2026-04-26._

When Slug fills a spot (replacing Peej), the map ratings change:

| Map | Change | Reason |
|-----|--------|--------|
| **Coastline** | ➡️ Roughly unchanged | Slug 50.0% Y11S1 (10M — confirmed at larger sample) vs Peej's 30.0% — Slug is clearly better here, but 50% is neutral rather than a strength; Coastline stays ⚠️ Situational. First half only (leaves at mid-season) |
| **Bank** | ⬇️ Downgrade | Slug 33.3% Y11S1 (9M) vs Peej's 58.3% — was 66.7% (3M small sample) previously; now confirmed below average at real volume. Bank drops further with Slug than with Peej |
| **Oregon** | ➡️ Roughly unchanged | Slug 42.9% Y11S1 (14M) vs Peej's 33.3% — Slug slightly better but both are below 50%; Sarge (26.7%) and Hound (30%) remain the team anchors of the problem. Oregon stays ❌ Avoid |
| **Nighthaven** | ⬇️ Moderate downgrade | Slug 37.5% Y11S1 (8M) vs Peej's 50.0% — Slug improved from 16.7% (6M) last pull but still noticeably below Peej; Nighthaven becomes weaker team-wide with Slug |
| **Villa** | ⬇️ Downgrade | Slug 38.5% Y11S1 (13M) vs Peej's 71.4% — Peej is a major positive on Villa; replacing with Slug pushes Villa toward avoid territory (Sarge 40%, Hound 37.5%, Smigs 37.5% all also below 40%) |
| **Skyscraper** | ✅ Stay solid | Slug all-time strong here — pairs with Hound's 62.5%; no downgrade from default lineup |

**ATK note:** Slug does not bring the Peej entry fragger identity (Ash/Brava). ATK structure shifts — no dedicated entry fragger in the lineup. Grant and Sarge absorb more of the entry/flex burden. Slug's ATK bright spots are Zero (30r / 66.7%) and Capitão (15r / 66.7%) — both self-directed and intel-heavy rather than team-entry focused. Adjust formation expectations accordingly.

**Adjusted ban priority with Slug:** Emerald Plains stays Ban 1. Nighthaven is still a strong Ban 2 candidate with Slug — Slug's 37.5% is better than before but still below Peej's 50%, and Hound's 12.5%/8M remains the team anchor. Villa should be moved toward ban consideration with Slug in — Slug 38.5% stacks on Sarge 40% / Hound 37.5% / Smigs 37.5%; only Grant (50%) holds even. Oregon/Consulate remain ❌ Avoid.

---

## Scenario: Hunter Fills In (B Team + Main Stack Mix)

When Hunter fills a spot, the map ratings change:

| Map | Change | Reason |
|-----|--------|--------|
| **Oregon** | ⬇️ Flip to avoid | Hunter 25.0% WR career (8M) — team's strongest protect becomes a liability with Hunter in the lineup |
| **Coastline** | ⬇️ Strengthen avoid | Hunter 36.4% WR (11M) — stacks on top of team's already-situational read on this map |
| **Villa** | ➡️ Comparable weakness | Hunter 40.0% WR (15M) — team is already ❌ Avoid on Villa in Y11S1; Hunter doesn't change the map rating |
| **Border** | ⬇️ Slight downgrade | Hunter 42.1% WR (19M) — was a clear team strength; slightly softer with Hunter |
| **Bank** | ⬇️ Slight downgrade | Hunter 42.9% WR (14M) — was a solid team keep; Hunter is just below 50% career here |

**Adjusted ban priority with Hunter:** Ban 1 stays Nighthaven (second half) or Emerald Plains (first half) — unchanged. Ban 2 shifts significantly — Oregon becomes a legitimate ban option since Hunter's 25% WR (8M) stacks on top of the team's already-mixed Y11S1 record there (Sarge 36.4%, Hound 33.3%). Coastline (first half only) is the alternative if Oregon isn't in the pool. Avoid putting Hunter on either.

**Note:** Hunter's DEF identity is strong across most maps — the WR damage above is primarily ATK-side drag. Maps where Hunter's DEF anchor ops (Aruni, Caveira, Valkyrie) are well-suited can partially offset the ATK-side weakness.

---

## Scenario: One Main Stack Player Missing (4+1)

When the 5th spot is filled by Bob or another B Team player, no set formula exists for the map veto — use the individual player's COACHING.md and ALL_TIME.md for their map context and factor it into the table above manually.

---

## Notes on Comfort and Frequency

The data in this file is a starting point. The comfort factor — "how do we feel about this map right now" — is a real signal and should override low-confidence data (small samples, maps not played recently). If the team played Clubhouse three times last week and felt good, that's relevant context the data can't capture.

Frequency also matters: maps the team plays more develop familiarity that improves WR over time. Banning a map forever prevents that development. Keep this in mind for maps in the "Weak" tier — they may be permanent bans (Chalet) or they may be development targets worth playing occasionally (Consulate, Nighthaven).

---

_For individual player map data, see each player's ALL_TIME.md and Y10S4.md files. For team formations on specific maps, see STACK_03_ATTACK_STRATS.md._
meta04Content:
# META_04 — Round Impact Score (RIS)

## Overview

The Round Impact Score (RIS) is a composite weighted metric designed to measure individual round-level contribution in a way that is fair across all playstyles and roles. It is not a pure kill metric — it rewards entry efficiency, combat output including assists, clutch performance, mechanical precision, and survivability in a balanced way.

RIS is expressed on a **0–100 scale** where **50.0 = average Gold-level player baseline**. Scores above 50 indicate above-average individual impact; scores below 50 indicate below-average. Typical range for this group is 30–65.

---

## Version History

| Version | Date | Changes |
|---|---|---|
| v1.0 | 2026-03-13 | Initial formula: ESR 35% / KDA 35% / Clutch 10% / HS% 15% / Survival 5% |
| v1.1 | 2026-03-21 | KDA slot → 70/30 KDA/KD blend; HS% reduced 15%→10%; Win Rate 5% added. Rationale: assists are damage-only (not camera/gadget), but KD still anchors raw kill efficiency. HS% diluted by gadget kills (Thorn, Kapkan, Fuze etc.). Win Rate rewards consistent winners across varied team contexts. |
| v1.2 | 2026-04-05 | HS% reduced 10%→8%; KDA* raised 35%→37%; clutch confidence damp threshold raised 30→60 matches. Rationale: HS% remains noisy for operator-diverse players (gadget kills can never be headshots, suppressing HS% for Thorn/Kapkan/Fuze/Maestro players); 8% keeps it as a tiebreaker. KDA* absorbs the freed 2% — it is the cleanest kill-efficiency signal available. Clutch damp raised because clutch events are rare (~1 per 5–7 matches); 30 matches yields only ~5 events, too few for a reliable rate; 60 matches (~9 events) is the minimum for statistical stability on low-frequency stats. |

---

## Formula (v1.2)

```
RIS = (ESR_score × 0.35) + (KDA_score × 0.37) + (WinRate_score × 0.05) + (Clutch_score × 0.10) + (HS_score × 0.08) + (Survival_score × 0.05)
      × 50 (scale to 0–100)

KDA_score = (KDA ÷ 1.35) × 0.70  +  (K/D ÷ 1.00) × 0.30   ← blended within the 37% KDA slot
```

Each component is normalised against a Gold-level baseline so that 1.0 = average:

| Component | Weight | Formula | Baseline |
|---|---|---|---|
| ESR | 35% | ESR ÷ 0.50 | 0.50 (break-even) |
| KDA/KD Blend | 37% | (KDA ÷ 1.35 × 0.70) + (K/D ÷ 1.00 × 0.30) | KDA 1.35 / K/D 1.00 |
| Win Rate | 5% | WR ÷ 0.50 | 0.50 (50% fair matchmaking baseline) |
| Clutch | 10% | (CW÷M × CWR) ÷ 0.015, capped at 3×, dampened by min(M÷60, 1.0) | 0.015 per match |
| HS% | 8% | HS% ÷ 45% | 45% |
| Survival | 5% | (Rounds − Deaths) ÷ Rounds ÷ 0.40 | 40% survival rate |

---

## Component Explanations

**ESR — Entry Success Rate (35%)**
The ratio of first kills to total first-contact events. Winning the first duel of a round creates an immediate 5v4 — it is the highest-leverage individual action in Siege. Break-even is 0.50. Above 0.50 = net positive entry contribution. Below 0.50 = net liability in entry situations.

**KDA/KD Blend — Combat Efficiency (35%)**
The KDA slot holds a blended score: 70% KDA + 30% K/D, both normalised against their respective Gold-level baselines. KDA ((Kills + Assists) ÷ Deaths) rewards damage-dealing assists — these are confirmed as damage-only on r6.tracker (camera assists, drone spots, and gadget setups do not count toward the tracker's assist stat). The 30% K/D anchor ensures that raw kill performance still has a direct signal weight — kills are more decisive than assists in most round outcomes, so a player with high assists but few kills does not fully escape the scrutiny of K/D. The blend captures both the team-play value of assists and the mechanical impact of getting kills.

**Clutch Factor (10%)**
Clutch wins are the only individual action that literally converts a lost round into a won round. Calculated as (Clutches Won ÷ Matches) × Clutch Win Rate, then normalised against a Gold-level average of 0.015 per match. Capped at 3× baseline to prevent extreme outliers from distorting the score. Additionally, a **confidence dampening** factor of `min(Matches ÷ 60, 1.0)` is applied after the cap — clutch wins are rare events (~1 per 5–7 matches), meaning a 30-match season yields only ~5 clutch events, far too few for a reliable rate estimate. The threshold was raised from 30 to 60 in v1.2 to require a minimum of ~9 events before treating the clutch rate as statistically stable. At 60+ matches the dampening resolves to 1.0 (no effect).

**Win Rate (5%)**
Match win rate normalised against a 50% fair-matchmaking baseline. Rewards players who consistently play on the winning side across varied team compositions. Weighted at 5% — meaningful but intentionally small, since win rate is team-influenced and partially outside individual control.

**HS% — Headshot Percentage (8%)**
A mechanical quality and aim precision indicator. Higher HS% correlates with faster kills (more decisive duels) and more intentional targeting. Normalised against 45% Gold-level average. Reduced from 15% to 10% in v1.1, then further to 8% in v1.2 — gadget kills (Thorn Razorbloom, Kapkan EDD, Fuze cluster, Maestro Evil Eye) count as kills but can never be headshots, diluting HS% for utility-heavy operators. At 8% it functions as a meaningful tiebreaker without materially penalising players whose operator pools include high gadget-kill volume.

**Survival Rate (5%)**
The percentage of rounds a player was alive at round-end. Weighted deliberately light because we cannot distinguish good deaths (plant-and-die, trade sacrifice) from bad ones (early panic peek). At 5% it acts as a minor positive signal for staying alive rather than a significant penalty for dying in-role.

---

## Data Sources

| Period | Source |
|---|---|
| Y11S1 | `Scripts/data/Y11S1_YYYY-MM-DD.json` — ESR, KDA, HS%, Clutches, ClutchWR, Kills, Deaths, Op arrays |
| Y10S4 | `Knowledge Base/PLAYERS/{Name}/Y10S4.md` — Core Stats table + Op arrays for round totals |
| All-Time | ❌ Not available — r6.tracker.network does not expose ESR, KDA, or Clutch at the career/lifetime level. Only K/D, Win%, HS%, and Matches are available lifetime. |

**Career Average RIS** is computed instead: a match-weighted average of all seasonal RIS scores. This is not true All-Time but reflects relative career trajectory from tracked seasons. See Score History table.

**Total Rounds** is derived by summing all operator round counts from the ATK + DEF operator arrays. If this sum is less than Deaths (data gap), `Matches × 6.5` is used as a fallback estimate.

**Assists** are derived from KDA and Deaths: `Assists = (KDA × Deaths) − Kills`

---

## Score Interpretation

| RIS | Interpretation |
|---|---|
| 65+ | Elite individual impact — top-tier round contribution |
| 55–64 | Above average — meaningful positive contribution |
| 45–54 | Near baseline — average Gold-level performance |
| 35–44 | Below average — some weaknesses pulling the score down |
| <35 | Significant drag — multiple components below baseline |

---

## Known Limitations

- **ESR is a ratio, not a rate.** We know *quality* of entry contacts (win rate) but not *frequency* (how often a player takes first contact per round). A player who only takes entry contact 5% of rounds looks identical in ESR to one doing it 40% of rounds. Entry rate would require raw opening kill/death counts which are not currently stored in the JSON schema.
- **No plants or defuses.** R6 Tracker does not expose plant/defuse counts. The closest proxy is ATK Win Rate, which is partially captured through KDA assists.
- **No trade tracking.** A trade kill (die but take one with you) is indistinguishable from a wasted death in this formula.
- **Win Rate is team-influenced.** A player on a weaker team composition will have a lower WR regardless of individual contribution. At 5% weight this is intentionally minor. Cross-season comparisons may be affected by team composition changes.
- **HS% still affected by gadget kills.** At 8% weight (reduced from 15% → 10% → 8% across v1.0–v1.2) this dilution is less impactful, but Thorn Razorbloom kills, Kapkan EDDs, Fuze cluster kills, and Maestro Evil Eye kills still suppress HS% for utility-heavy operators. The reduction mitigates but does not eliminate the bias.
- **Krafty Y11S1 ESR.** ESR was 0.0 in the initial 2026-03-13 pull (12M — data gap likely). Updated to 0.13 in the 2026-03-20 repull (47M), 0.15 in the 2026-03-21 repull (58M — now reliable sample). KDA/KD blend (0.389) and ESR (0.15) are both real and consistent.
- **Small samples flagged.** Players with <30 matches in a given season are flagged ⚠️. Scores are directionally valid but not statistically reliable. The clutch confidence dampening (`min(M÷60, 1.0)`) reduces clutch contribution proportionally below 60M — at 60 matches a player has had ~9 clutch events, the minimum for a stable rate estimate.

---

## Score History

_Y11S1 scores are RIS v1.2. Y10S4 scores are v1.0 — direct cross-season Δ comparison is approximate due to formula changes._

| Player | Y10S4 (v1.0) | Y11S1 (v1.2) | Δ (prev pull) | Career Avg† | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Smigs | 57.8 | 59.7 | +0.8 | 58.6 | Platinum IV; 60M. ESR 0.59 + Thermite/Kaid core steady |
| Peej | 55.5 | 59.2 | −1.1 | 57.7 | Emerald V; 99M. Caveira/Thorn DEF pairing strong; Brava ATK rising |
| Grant | 57.1 | 55.9 | +0.3 | 56.4 | Emerald I; 178M. Mute/Kapkan/Castle/Thorn DEF anchor holds |
| Sarge | 56.1 | 54.1 | −0.2 | 55.1 | Platinum IV; 132M. Zofia volume steady; Vigil/Azami DEF holding |
| Slug | 51.9 | 47.8 | +1.3 | 50.5 | Emerald V; 107M. Zero/Valkyrie standouts; ESR still drag *(B Team)* |
| Hound | 46.5 | 42.9 | −0.3 | 45.0 | Emerald IV; 104M. Wide op spread; ESR drag on formula |
| Hunter | N/A | 42.2 | +6.5 | 42.2 ⚠️ | Gold I; 58M. Big jump — Aruni DEF anchor emerging *(B Team)* |
| Bob | 37.1 | 32.5 | −0.5 | 34.0 | Silver I; 75M. WR drag — Kapkan/Denari/Thorn DEF showing traction *(B Team)* |
| Krafty | 30.2 | 23.3 | +0.3 | 25.7 | Silver V; 116M. ESR + KDA/KD blend both confirmed drags *(B Team)* |
| TazRathmus | N/A | 19.2 ⚠️ | 0.0 | 19.2 ⚠️ | Copper III; 4M. Insufficient sample — no meaningful signal yet *(C Team)* |

_Baseline: 50.0 (average Gold-level player)_
_Pull dates: Y10S4 verified 2026-03-11 · Y11S1 repulled 2026-04-26 (178/99/104/60/132/107/116/75/58/4M)_
_† Career Avg = match-weighted average across tracked seasons only (Y10S4 + Y11S1). Hunter/TazRathmus Y10S4 insufficient for RIS — Y11S1 only. Δ (prev pull) = change from 2026-04-10 Y11S1 pull. Cross-version comparisons approximate._
_True All-Time not available — r6.tracker.network does not expose ESR/KDA/Clutch at career level. Investigated 2026-03-14._

---

## Update Protocol

RIS scores are calculated from the same data pull workflow used for all other seasonal stats. No additional browser pulls are required — all inputs are collected in the standard seasonal JSON.

After each seasonal data pull:
1. Run `node Scripts/compute_ris.js Scripts/data/<season>_<date>.json --update`
   - Reads the JSON, prints component breakdown, writes `| RIS |` row into each player's season `.md` file
2. Update the Score History table above with the new scores and Δ values
3. Recompute Career Avg (match-weighted across all tracked seasons)

**compute_ris.js flags:**
- `--update` — writes RIS row to each player's season .md file (after ESR row)
- `--season <LABEL>` — overrides the season file to target (e.g. `--season ALL_TIME`)
- Player names as extra args — filter to specific players only

coachingItems:
3. **Sarge rank gap** — conversation about solo queue vs. stack performance discrepancy
4. **Peej ATK op balance (Y11S1)** — Ash (65r / 38.5%) is the volume pick but underperforming relative to season expectations. Brava (33r / 54.5%) is outperforming Ash by a wide margin with a meaningful sample. Worth experimenting with Brava as the default ATK pick on maps where DEF gadget destruction is valuable, rather than defaulting to Ash every round. Also note Twitch (55r / 43.6%) — confirm the team is getting value from the drone-disable intel role, not just using it as a generic soft-breacher. Caveira DEF (90r / 64.4%) is elite — protect that role.
5. **Hound ATK op pool (Y11S1)** — All top-6 ATK ops are underperforming: Thermite (40r / 35%), Osa (38r / 36.8%), Nomad (28r / 32.1%), Fuze (23r / 39.1%), Thatcher (22r / 41.4%), Zero (22r / 45.5%). Rauora (15r / 53.3%) and Gridlock (11r / 72.7%) are the clear bright spots. Consider shifting volume toward Rauora as a primary ATK pick — the sample is meaningful and the WR gap is significant. ATK improvement is the biggest lever for Hound's overall performance.

---

coachingItemsStructured.body: conversation about solo queue vs. stack performance discrepancy

Ash (65r / 38.5%) is the volume pick but underperforming relative to season expectations. Brava (33r / 54.5%) is outperforming Ash by a wide margin with a meaningful sample. Worth experimenting with Brava as the default ATK pick on maps where DEF gadget destruction is valuable, rather than defaulting to Ash every round. Also note Twitch (55r / 43.6%) — confirm the team is getting value from the drone-disable intel role, not just using it as a generic soft-breacher. Caveira DEF (90r / 64.4%) is elite — protect that role.

All top-6 ATK ops are underperforming: Thermite (40r / 35%), Osa (38r / 36.8%), Nomad (28r / 32.1%), Fuze (23r / 39.1%), Thatcher (22r / 41.4%), Zero (22r / 45.5%). Rauora (15r / 53.3%) and Gridlock (11r / 72.7%) are the clear bright spots. Consider shifting volume toward Rauora as a primary ATK pick — the sample is meaningful and the WR gap is significant. ATK improvement is the biggest lever for Hound's overall performance.

teamFocusItems.text: Attack win rate is the gap.
teamFocusItems.body: The team wins on defense and loses rounds on attack — all 5 players have strong DEF stats. Every session, identify at least one ATK-side adjustment (timing, formation, or operator) to close the gap. Even a 5–10% improvement in ATK WR translates directly to rank gains.

teamFocusItems.text: Protect your best maps in the ban phase.
teamFocusItems.body: Clubhouse, Chalet, and Border are the current strength maps. Don't give them up in the ban phase. Let the opponent ban their own bad maps — your job is to protect your wins.

teamFocusItems.text: Entry timing is a team action, not a solo one.
teamFocusItems.body: Peej leads entry with Ash — but entry only works when breach detonation and utility are deployed first. Hard breach goes before Ash pushes. If Peej is dying on entry, ask whether breach/utility preceded the push.

When callouts break down, each player makes defensive reads in isolation and the defense collapses. Prioritize active comms through plant phase and post-plant. The team's DEF strength comes from coordination, not just individual anchors.

teamFocusItems.text: Emerald Plains is a hard ban — no exceptions.
teamFocusItems.body: Universal 0% WR across all five players. Do not negotiate it away in the ban phase regardless of opponent tendencies. This is the team's unambiguous worst map.

teamFocusItems.text: Oregon and Consulate are now confirmed team-wide weaknesses.
teamFocusItems.body: Oregon: 4 of 5 players below 50% with Sarge (28.6%/14M) and Hound (30%/10M) confirmed at scale. Consulate: all five players at 20–27% with 5–11M each — the most conclusive team-wide signal in the pool. Treat both as priority Ban 2 candidates. Villa is no longer a ban target — Grant (53.8%) and Peej (83.3%) are both clearly positive there.
